page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 150 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the instance exhibits lower-tier compute and memory performance. In stress-ng and Geekbench tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10% category. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only small and large block operations, are classified as weak, placing in the bottom 25%, while large block write operations drop to the bottom 10%. However, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates average performance in the PassMark CPU encryption test, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

The primary tradeoff of the r5ad.xlarge is its modest processing speed and memory throughput relative to its high memory capacity and local NVMe storage. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high density of DDR4 memory and fast local storage without the premium associated with newer generation processors, though it is constrained by a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads that require local scratch space, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and basic data processing tasks that do not demand high CPU throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz, this x86_64 instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor. While it offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, benchmark data shows that its compute and memory bandwidth performance generally fall into the bottom 10% to 25% cohorts, with the exception of average performance in CPU encryption tests. The instance is configured with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It is designed for memory-intensive applications requiring local temporary storage, such as caching and moderate database workloads, where high CPU throughput is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB NVMe SSD. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 CPU and is suited for memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 150 GB NVMe SSD, powered by an AMD EPYC CPU.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance pairing dedicated AMD EPYC cores with local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in hardware-accelerated CPU encryption tests
Suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching, and lightweight databases
